function.name: memory_init
function.description: Initialize ultra-memory session. Call at conversation start to create three-layer memory structure and inject historical context.

resume.description: Try to resume the most recent session for this project

function.name: memory_status
function.description: Query current session status: operation count, last milestone, context pressure level (low/medium/high/critical). Use to decide when to compress.

function.name: memory_log
function.description: Record an operation to the current session log (Layer 1). Call after every significant action: file write, bash command, tool call, decision, error, or milestone.

function.name: memory_recall
function.description: Retrieve relevant records from all memory layers (ops log, summary, semantic, entity index). Use when you need to recall past work, decisions, or errors.

function.name: memory_summarize
function.description: Trigger summary compression of the current session (compresses ops log into summary.md). Call when context pressure is high or critical, or every ~50 operations.

force.description: Force compression even if operation count is low

function.name: memory_restore
function.description: Restore the last session context for a project. Use at conversation start to continue a previous task across days.

function.name: memory_profile
function.description: Read or update user profile (tech stack, preferences, project list). Persist user-level preferences across all sessions.

function.name: memory_entities
function.description: Query structured entity index (functions, files, dependencies, decisions, errors). Use for precise questions like 'which functions were used', 'which packages were installed', 'what decisions were made'.

query.description: Search keyword (optional, empty returns all of that type)

function.name: memory_extract_entities
function.description: Re-extract all structured entities from a session's ops.jsonl. Use to repair or initialize the entity index.
